competitive_position: No external competitor. Publicis selected Zycus sole-source without an RFP. Without Merlin as core, the AI differentiation weakens and the quick-win timeline is at risk.

competitive_position: Conga (Salesforce CLM) is the single strongest threat — the EB-sponsored incumbent the RFP exists to test, positioned as lower-risk 'enhance vs replace.' Zycus wins on external-legal Word redlining, clause library, risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, and D365/Merlin AI. Coupa is dormant; DocuSign/Certinal appear only as comparison-case-study asks. Do-nothing/enhance-Conga is the active fallback to beat on differentiation and price.

champion.summary: Michael Tutton is a strong, conviction-driven champion credited with getting the RFP launched, but he lacks power-line access to skeptical EB Bevan and admits no prior Zycus experience — making him the EB-broker is the highest-leverage use of his strength.

items.detail: 21-day post-proposal window (10 Jul → 31 Jul) cannot fit selection, external-counsel redlines, InfoSec, and signature; buyer's prior mid-Feb target already slipped 4+ months. No MSA/SOW or InfoSec pre-work started.

No pricing, TCO, or ROI model delivered across 13 calls; Bevan is cost-sensitive ('how many times do I get nickeled and dimed', 16 Oct 2025) and price is a major RFP scoring factor. This is the top winnability risk.

EB Bevan skeptical and never directly engaged; risk/legal exec 'Jonathan' never contacted. Deal rests on a single objective champion who cannot move the EB alone — must be brokered through him, not bypassed.
External counsel will redline Zycus paper; no MSA/SOW exchanged or pre-aligned, no InfoSec contact.

Conga incumbent with EB-level sponsorship and lower-risk 'enhance vs replace' positioning; if RFP scoring does not heavily weight Zycus's CLM/AI/integration advantages, Conga may win on 'good enough' + price. Comparison/ROI requested 03 Sep 2025, still undelivered.
D365 ERP integration is in scope but never technically scoped; no API/implementation plan — a post-award delay risk.

recommended_moves: Win the proposal on differentiation + price: build the RFP response around the exact Conga gaps ARUP codified (external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, D365/Merlin) AND embed a bundled TCO/ROI model that answers the RFP pricing questions (power vs business users, e-sign envelope volume, supplier counts).

Pre-stage paper and InfoSec in parallel with the proposal: name a Zycus paper owner, send the standard MSA/SOW/Order Form and a SOC2/security packet to Mzia/Michael for routing to external counsel, and confirm the InfoSec process.

Arm Michael to broker the leadership conversation: equip him with the differentiation-led Conga comparison, ROI narrative, and Gartner positioning to take to Bevan and the risk/legal exec ('Jonathan') so the value case reaches power through the champion who authored the RFP.

items.detail: RFP bake-off with 3 unknown competitors and no clear lead position yet. Zycus is confirmed as one of 4 finalists (15 May) but has not differentiated enough to be the sole front-runner. The buyer's selection is best-fit within budget (not cheapest), which advantages Zycus's unified AI platform story, but the competitive field is unknown and the RFP scoring (responses due 30 June, 4 days out) will determine the outcome. Risk: a competitor with a more surgical RFP response, a stronger CFO business case narrative, or a lower-price bid could win if Zycus does not lead on fit and value.
items.status: Active. RFP responses due 30 June (4 days out). Mitigation: deliver a focused RFP response on Sitecore's pain points (AP automation, Microsoft integration, unified platform) and co-author the CFO business case to embed differentiation into the internal sell.

items.detail: The Salesforce close date (30 July, 34 days out) is ahead of the buyer's realistic decision and contracting timeline. Elie's stated timeline (15 May call) is: RFP responses due 30 June, evaluation early July, business case to CFO mid-July, decision end of July, then contract negotiation and transition August-March 2027. That timeline implies contract close in August, not 30 July. If Zycus forecasts to 30 July and the buyer's process runs to its natural pace, the deal will slip 2-4 weeks, creating internal forecast miss and pressure on the deal team.
items.status: Active. Buyer timeline (decision end July, contract close August) diverges from Salesforce close date (30 July). Mitigation: align internal forecast to August and work with the buyer to accelerate CFO approval and contracting if possible, but do not push the buyer to compress a well-structured process.

items.detail: No CFO engagement yet, and the CFO is the final budget approver. Elie will present the business case to the CFO by mid-July (target ~15 July, 19 days out), but Zycus has not been introduced to the CFO, has not shaped the business case narrative, and does not know the CFO's decision criteria or risk concerns. If the business case is built in isolation, it may default to a price-driven comparison or miss the CFO's priorities (e.g. PE cost discipline, ROI proof, implementation risk). Risk: the CFO rejects or delays the decision, or chooses a lower-cost competitor, because Zycus's value story was not embedded in the business case.
items.status: Active. CFO business case presentation targeted mid-July (19 days out); Zycus not yet engaged in shaping it. Mitigation: proactively offer to co-author the ROI model and business case with Elie, request a CFO intro or joint session to align on value, and provide Paul Hastings reference data to de-risk the decision.

items.detail: Migration complexity from a mature 5-6 year Coupa deployment is a stated buyer concern. Elie said (9 April): 'the ease of doing that, we're really gonna need to convince people, especially leadership. I think it's the right time.' Sitecore has a well-established P2P process with users accustomed to Coupa, and leadership buy-in for the migration (which includes budget impact, implementation timeline, and change management load) is required. If migration plan is not detailed and de-risked, leadership may defer the decision or choose to renew Coupa for another year.
items.status: Acknowledged but not yet mitigated. items.detail: Zero buyer contact: no calls, no confirmed stakeholders, no champion, no EB/DM mapped. The deal is a one-way RFI submission with no relationship, no advocacy, and no intelligence on competition or buyer priorities. If competitors have established buyer relationships and we are responding cold, win probability is very low regardless of product fit.

Single-thread risk with zero redundancy. If the one unnamed RFI point-of-contact is a junior procurement officer with no influence, or if they favor a competitor or incumbent, we have no alternative thread to the decision-makers or Economic Buyer. Public-sector deals often have multi-stakeholder tender committees; being locked out of that committee is a political vulnerability.

Competitive blind spot: responding to an RFI without knowing who else is in the shortlist or how SARS scores vendors risks a generic, undifferentiated response that does not counter competitor strengths or emphasize our Merlin wedge where it matters most.

Zero buyer relationship: no calls, no contact roles, no champion, no EB/DM mapped. Responding to a government RFI cold with no relationship intelligence, no advocate inside the tender committee, and no visibility into competition or decision criteria is the single largest vulnerability. If competitors have established buyer relationships and have shaped the RFI or have incumbent advantage, Zycus's win probability is very low regardless of product fit.

Competitive blind spot: no intelligence on who else is shortlisted, whether SARS has an incumbent (SAP Ariba or SAP ERP), or how competitors are positioning. Responding to an RFI without knowing competitor strengths or how SARS scores vendors risks a generic, undifferentiated response that does not counter competitor advantages or emphasize the Merlin wedge where it matters most.

SARS is a government revenue agency. InfoSec, data-residency (POPIA compliance, South African data sovereignty), and audit/compliance requirements are likely critical gates, but we have no visibility into what the RFI specifies or whether Zycus can meet them (e.g. local data hosting, government cloud certification). A non-negotiable requirement we cannot meet would disqualify us post-submission.

Public-sector procurements in South Africa may require or prefer a local systems-integration partner or reseller for implementation, support, and government relationship management. If the RFP mandates local presence and Zycus has not engaged a South African SI/channel partner (beyond Letsema supporting role noted in CRM), the proposal may be non-compliant or scored lower than competitors with established local partnerships.

competitive_position: Jagger (incumbent, 8 years) is OUT — already displaced by signed Zycus S2P phase-one contract, lost on product stagnation and vendor disengagement. Do-nothing/delay is the LIVE competitive threat (highest current risk): buyer's stated adoption timeline is 'late 27–28 initiative' with 1–2 year org-wide rollout due to legacy-user resistance, InfoSec gatekeeping, and multi-ERP complexity. Zycus is sole-source for Merlin Intake (no peer vendor evaluation), but organizational readiness constraints and 'last-mile AI adoption' challenges (Alex, 12 Jun) create deprioritization risk. Wedge: prove adoption lift in a Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 pilot with indirect sourcing power users, leverage Scott Anderson's ISM-demo enthusiasm and Horizon peer validation (Sept 21–23) to create executive pull, and position Merlin Intake as the 'interface-agnostic, no-training' UX solution that solves Jagger's failure and de-risks the broader 2027–2028 rollout.

items.detail: Close date 2026-07-24 (28 days out) is fundamentally misaligned with buyer's stated adoption timeline ('late 27–28 initiative', 18-month horizon per Alex, call 2026-06-12). Remaining steps (Nathan Hemmer strategic approval, EB/budget mapping, commercial discussion, contract/SOW, InfoSec approval for Teams app deployment, user pilot) cannot complete in 28 days. Best Case forecast is indefensible.

Organizational readiness and user adoption are multi-year challenges. Buyer articulated: (1) legacy power users resist chat interface ('I can fill this form out within 15 seconds… that's just gonna slow me down'), (2) cultural shift to AI-enabled workflows will take time ('organization-wide adoption expected to take ~1–2 years'), (3) InfoSec and IT gatekeeping on AI tools (Copilot, cloud, chat interfaces) slowing rollout ('IT being okay with information security'), (4) 'last-mile AI adoption problem… we spent billions of dollars for AI infrastructure and we are still having issues seeing the impact on P&L' (Alex, 12 Jun call). Risk: do-nothing/delay is highest competitive threat. Mitigation: phased rollout starting with small pilot (indirect sourcing power users, Q4 2026 or Q1 2027) to prove adoption lift and de-risk broader 2027/2028 rollout.
items.status: Active; pilot design and adoption proof are the path forward.
items.first_raised: Alex articulated adoption challenges on demo call

items.detail: Strategic decision-maker Nathan Hemmer ('Nate', Sr. Corp. Director of Indirect Procurement Global) has not engaged on any call. Alex is gathering information and will report findings to Nathan, who will 'set the strategic sail' (Alex, call 2026-06-12). Risk: deal stalls in exploration mode if Nathan does not confirm strategic intent or prioritize Merlin Intake adoption. No EB, no budget-holder, no approval-chain mapped.

Multi-ERP complexity (5 ERPs, 20–30 business units) prevents centralized supplier onboarding and limits orchestrated multi-step workflows (sourcing → contract → supplier onboarding → PR) that Zycus demoed. Buyer stated 'suppliers have to exist in the ERP system to flow from the requisition to the purchase order… we cannot do supplier onboarding' and 'it's hard to visualize how to realize it with how we are set up today' (Alex, 12 Jun call). Zycus acknowledged and confirmed the flow can be scoped to existing-supplier/catalog/punchout paths only. Risk: advanced AI orchestration value proposition is partially blocked by integration constraints. Mitigation: scope Merlin Intake to catalog/punchout/existing-supplier paths (which Alex validated as valuable) and defer multi-step orchestration to future phase once ERP/supplier-data landscape simplifies.
items.status: Acknowledged and mitigated; scope confirmed as catalog/punchout/existing-supplier only.

items.detail: InfoSec and IT approval required for deploying Microsoft Teams app (Merlin Intake) and enabling AI/chat-based requisitioning. Mitigation: map IT/InfoSec stakeholders, initiate security review early (data handling, Teams app certification, AI/LLM data residency), and leverage Zycus's Microsoft Teams certified-app status and existing Fortive S2P deployment as trust anchor.
